title: Fatty, Oozy Portuguese Lamb Gratin
description: A rich and comforting Portuguese dish featuring oregano, with a crispy crust and gooey interior, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Season the lamb cubes with salt and black pepper.
In a large Dutch oven, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter to the pot and melt over medium heat.
Add the flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Gradually add the chicken broth and red wine, whisking continuously.
Return the lamb to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and bake for 25-30 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and sprinkle the chopped oregano over the top.
Bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the surface is golden brown and the lamb is tender.
Serve hot, garnished with additional oregano if desired.
